meaning: possessive suffix pronoun of the 1st person
origin_explanation: SEMITIC: for this suffix pronoun in the Semitic languages, see Brockelmann 1908:307-8; Barth 1913: 35ff. ETHIOPIC: Tna. -y, -ay, Te. -ye, Amh.Arg. -yä, Gur. -(a)y; contracted into -e in Amh.Arg.Har. Gurage. For other Ethiopian languages, see Leslau 1956: 57.
from soba, sobe (see above), with the suffix -ya.

phonetics: ye, ye ye, yo, yo yo
meaning: exclamation of admiration (oh!), of grief or pain ('alas!, ah!')
origin_explanation: SEMITIC: Ar. yā + l + suffix pronoun 'oh what!' (admiration), Ug. y 'woe!'. ETHIOPIC: Te. yo, yāh, yāw 'woe!, oh!', Tna. ye, yāy, yāye, yāh, Amh. ye, ayä, exclamation of surprise, wonder, or sorrow.

meaning: 'she, this, that, same, the'; yəʾəta gize 'then'; yəʾəta soba 'right then'; zəyəʾəti 'that is to say'; wəyəʾəti 'namely'; also serves as copula: e.g. 'ana yəʾəti 'I am', yəʾəti zətti 'this is'
origin_explanation: SEMITIC: Ar. (Spain) hiet 'she', Ar. (Palestine) hite, SAr. hyt. For the final -tu, -ti, see wəʾətu, above. For the other Semitic languages, see Brockelmann 1908:302-4; Barth 1913:14-8. ETHIOPIC: Te. həta, Gaf. yət, Gur. x'it, x'ita, it; for other Ethiopian languages, see Leslau 1979:359, under kiya.

meaning: 'now, just now, at the moment, at present'
origin_explanation: with a negative verb, 'not yet'; wəyəʾəze-ssa 'but now'; wəyəʾəze-ni 'now then, and now'; ʾəskə yəʾəze 'till now'; ʾəmyəʾəze 'from this time forward, from now on, henceforth'; SEMITIC: Dillmann 1907:377 analyzes it as yəʾə-ze; he identifies (p. 121) yəʾə with yəʾə- of yəʾəti 'she', and compares -ze with zə of zəya 'there' and with Heb. ʾəzay 'then' (p. 377, n. 2). Barth 1893:59-60 analyzes yəʾəze as yə, originally the prefix of the 3rd person, imperfect (comparing it with various elements, such as Ar. ʾayy 'which?', G. ʾayy, but he does not explain the element ʾof yəʾə), and compares -ze with -ze of məʾəze 'when?', zəya 'there'. Torczyner 67 compares Ar. ʾidā 'then', Aram. ʾādayin 'until now, still' (which, however, is interpreted as coming from ʾad hēn, ʾad henā 'until now'). Eitan, AJSL 46 (1929-30). 39 explains yəʾəze as coming from a deictic element yə- and *ʾəze which he, like Dillmann, identifies with Heb. ʾəzay. Rundgren 1955:44 and Littmann 1964:374 compare -ze of yəʾəze with G. gize (from gi-ze) 'time'. Baumgartner 26 compares yəʾəze with Ar. ʾid 'then'. ETHIOPIC: Tna. həzi, həgi 'now', Te. ʾəze, Har. hōgi, Gur. awǧe, awǧi (with palatalization of z > *ž > ǧ).
formally it is the imperfect of bəhla, above.

meaning: 'jubilate, shout with joy, rejoice, exult, triumph, (Gr.311) applaud'; yəbbābəbe 'jubilation, cries of joy'
origin_explanation: SEMITIC: Ar. ʾabbaba 'shout', Heb. yibbeb 'lament', Aram. yəbab 'shout an alarm', Syr. yəbbəb 'make a joyful noise, howl (as the wind)', Md. ybb 'groan, whistle'. ETHIOPIC: Amh. yäbbäbä 'rejoice, exult' (from Geez). See also babbaya.
from Gk. ἴβις, acc. sg. of ἴβις 'ibis'. See also ἴbn.

meaning: 'be dry, be arid, dry up, be withered (hand)'
origin_explanation: SEMİTİC: Ar. yəbisa 'be dry', SAR. ybs., Heb. yābeš, Aram. yābeš, Syr. ἴbeš, Md. ybš. ETHIOPIC: Te. yābaṣa 'be dry', Tna. yābas 'dry land', Amh. yabs (from Geez).
since T is the only source for yādə one wonders whether it is a misprint or a misunderstanding of yādən (below), and Amh. roman would then stand for romay 'Latin' indicating the origin of yādən.

meaning: ayda 'make know, inform, tell, declare, announce, proclaim, report, relate'
origin_explanation: SEMİTİC: Ar. ayda'a 'inform' (see Noeldeke 1910:202-3), Sar. yd 'find out', hyd 'inform', Soq. yədəh 'know', Šh. yədə' 'hear', Heb. yāda' 'Aram. yəda', Syr. idə', Md. ydə, Ph.Ug. yd', Akk. edū, idū, wadū. ETHIOPIC: See əndə'i, above.
from ἴdūn, Greek rendering of Latin ἴduum, gen. of ἴdūs 'Ides'.
one recognizes Ar. fā's 'axe', Amh. fas.

meaning: 'perhaps, possibly, by chance, per chance, peradventure, lest'
origin_explanation: Praetorius, ZDMG 47 (1893).390-1 derives it from Cushitic: Or. məgī 'perhaps', Som. moyi 'I don't know' (from the negative mā, and og 'know'), Geez yogi coming from ʔi-ogi. Geez yogi perhaps comes rather from Bil yigā 'but'.

meaning: proper name of a person or tribe (Judah, Judea)
origin_explanation: SEMİTİC: SAR. yhd 'Jews' (W. Müller), 'Judean, Jew'; for the Aramaic origin of see Jeffery 294, A. Fischer 1953:157. ETHIOPIC: Tna. yəhudi 'Jew', Te. yəhudanna 'Judaism', Amh. yəhudawi 'Jew'.

meaning: ayhə'a 'descend (from heaven), go down, fall down, bring down, make fall, (Lt) proceed'
origin_explanation: is to be connected with G. ayh 'flood', but the final is enigmatic.

meaning: 'jacinth (gem), cloth or garment spun with a purple thread'
origin_explanation: from Gk. hyakinthos 'jacinth'; also Amh. yakənt, yayqəntən, dəyāqəntən (see Krauss 212), Syr. yāqūndā (Noeldeke 1910:40), Ar. yāqūt 'ruby' (Jeffe ry 289, A. Fischer 1953:155). See also hayākəntos.
ETHIOPIC: Amh. yäkkäsä, täyäkkäsä 'ask', Tna. wä kāsä. For Amh. täyäkkäsä coming from *täyäqqäsä > *tyks > tyks, see Leslau 1979:628-9 under təqäsä.

meaning: sixth Ethiopian month (corresponding to 8 February-9 March)
origin_explanation: ETHIOPIC: Amh. yäkkätit, Tna. yäkkattit, läkkattit (for the variant y;l, cp. yäwḥe, läwḥe, under wäwəḥa; lomi, yomi, under yom); also in Cushitic: Sa. yäkkätit.
see also aktəyārān, yātəyārākəp.
see also yākiyārān, yātəyārākəp.

SEMİTİC: Ar. yawm 'day' (occasionally yam 'when'), Sar. ywm 'day, time', Soq. yōm, Heb. yōm 'day', yōmām 'daytime', Aram. yōmā 'day', ymā 'time', Syr. yawmā 'day', Md. ium, Ug. ym, imu. For this root, see also Noel deke 1910, ETHIOPIC: Te. yom 'today', Tna. lomi, lom (for l;y, see yäkkätit), Amh. yom 'day' (from Geez).

SEMİTİC: Ar. yamīn 'right', Sar. ymn, Šh. ymn), Heb. yāmīn, Aram.-Syr. yammīnā, Md. ug. ymn, Akk. imnu. ETHIOPIC: Tna. yämīn 'right', Te. man, dəmman (from *əd yämān 'right hand'), Amh. yämīn 'right hand' (from Geez).
from Gk. amēn, acc. of amē 'shovel'; or could it be a corrupt form of Heb. yā'īm 'shovels'? (W. Müller). See also iyyāmīn.
ETHIOPIC: Tna. yämāna 'very much'.
from Ar. yūnān 'Greeks' with the Geez suffix -āwi. See also yōnāyāwān, below.
seems to be an interpretation of a proper noun, perhaps Latin 'Ianus' (W. Müller).
an interpretation of the proper noun Jonah, Gk. Iōnas. See also wānos.
from Latin Jānuārius 'January'.
perhaps corruption of yonānāwī, above, with the plural marker -ān.
interpretation of the Greek proper name of a col umn Iachum, transcription of Heb. yākin, in 3 Kings 7:21 (= 1 Kings 7:21). See also balaz.
from rabha 'be wide, be large', with a prefixed y. For nouns formed with a prefixed y, see Barth 1894:226-233; Bauer, ZDMG 71 (1917).409.

meaning: 'a small bird, the bird which taught Yared the zemā-melodies'
origin_explanation: (or perhaps 'Yared, the bird', a simile in a poem). See ʿof.
perhaps a place-name.
from Gk. iaspis 'jasper'; also Te. yaspis. See also iyyāspis, above.

meaning: be gentle, be mild, be modest, be innocent, be submissive, show clemency, soothe, mitigate, charm, flatter, pacify, persuade, deceive'; 'charm with flattering words'; 'be gentle, be mild, let oneself be pacified or persuaded, be flattered'; 'flatter one another, be gentle with one another'; 'gentle, meek, innocent, mild, modest'; 'gentle, meek, simple, mild, innocent, modest, sincere, candid, (Lt) silly, humble, submissive, courteous, amiable'; 'mild, gentle'; '(yawwaha 'mildly, gently')
origin_explanation: SEMITIC: Ar. yayya'a 'show kindness, flatter', Soq. diye 'good' (from di-ye; see Leslau 1938:126), Heb. yād (yyy) 'be befitting', Aram. yā'e 'fitting, right', Syr. yā'e, ya(')yā 'fair, virtuous', Md. y', Ph. y' 'fine, fair'. ETHIOPIC: Amh. yāwwah 'meek, naive' (from Geez), Tna. yāwwaha, lāwwaha 'be meek, be gentle, be mild' (for y.l, see yakkā'it), Te. yawhe, lawha.

meaning: 'gentleness, mildness, simplicity, na-ivetē, meekness, modesty, innocence, serenity, peace, peacefulness'
'mildness, gentleness, innocence, sim-plicity, modesty, meekness, graciousness, tran-quility, integrity, serenity, (Lt) stupidity'
iota (the Greek letter), name given to Jesus (from the initial letter of his Greek name Iēsous), the Ten Commandments
origin_explanation: the Greek letter ι having the form of the Geez numeral ፲ 'ten'.
